messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-selection agent. Your task is to prepare a compact context pack for a later autoformalization agent. You see only source-side mathematical text plus prefix/local Lean context. The target Lean declaration name, statement, and proof are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "task": "For each record, sketch the formalization and select the tight local/Mathlib context a later proof-writing model should see. Prefer a few thousand tokens or less of Mathlib facts per record.",
  "round": "round1-formalization-sketch-and-context-needs",
  "required_json_schema": {
    "records": [
      {
        "record_key": "record-001",
        "source_focus_summary": "what precise source statement or part should be formalized",
        "selected_source_part": "the single declaration-level source part chosen for the withheld row; not a bundle of prior/supporting facts",
        "source_part_rationale": "why this one part was chosen, using source labels and prefix progress when available",
        "supporting_context_boundary": "which displayed prior/imported facts are support only and must not be restated or bundled into the target theorem",
        "formalization_sketch": [
          "Lean-level sketch of the likely statement shape and proof plan, without inventing hidden target names"
        ],
        "needed_local_context": [
          {
            "name_or_snippet": "displayed local declaration/notation/variable needed",
            "why_needed": "role in statement/proof"
          }
        ],
        "candidate_project_context": [
          {
            "name": "previous imported project declaration name, if useful",
            "expected_signature_or_shape": "signature shape if known from the provided context",
            "why_needed": "why this previous formalized statement belongs in the later context pack",
            "confidence": 0.0
          }
        ],
        "mathlib_queries": [
          "narrow Mathlib search phrase or expected declaration name, e.g. Nat.choose_symm"
        ],
        "candidate_mathlib_context": [
          {
            "name": "exact or likely Mathlib declaration name",
            "kind": "theorem|lemma|def|notation|tactic|typeclass|unknown",
            "expected_signature_or_shape": "signature shape if known",
            "why_needed": "why this belongs in the later context pack",
            "confidence": 0.0
          }
        ],
        "proof_notes": [
          "brief notes that may prevent API/type-signature mistakes"
        ],
        "uncertainties": [
          "what may require Mathlib/source lookup in a second round"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "selection_budget": {
    "max_added_context_tokens_per_record": 4000,
    "prefer_exact_signatures_over_long_files": true,
    "prefer_few_precise_facts_over_broad_import_dumps": true
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not ask for or infer the withheld target Lean declaration name, statement, or proof.",
    "Do not rely on legacy broad `Mathlib` imports as context; select concrete APIs/signatures/docstrings needed by the later generator.",
    "If a Mathlib name is uncertain, return a narrow search query plus the expected type/signature shape.",
    "Include previous formalized local declarations only if they are displayed in the prefix/local context.",
    "If `source_progress_context.imported_source_label_declarations` contains a previous imported project theorem matching the selected source part, include it in `candidate_project_context` and prefer it over reproving a long result.",
    "The benchmark rows are declaration-level targets aligned to source labels, not one row per LaTeX theorem environment. Select one likely target declaration, not every claim under the label.",
    "If `source_progress_context.same_label_progress_summary` or `prior_same_label_declarations` shows that earlier declarations already formalized source parts, do not re-formalize those parts or bundle them into a conjunction; select the remaining/next declaration-level part.",
    "Do not infer that the target should bundle all imported/prior same-label facts just because several such facts are available. Put reused facts in `candidate_project_context` and explain their boundary in `supporting_context_boundary`.",
    "Separate mathematical understanding from Lean API uncertainty in `uncertainties`.",
    "Do not over-select: every requested fact should have a role in statement typing or proof construction."
  ],

  "records": [
    {
      "record_key": "record-003",
      "source_statement_or_chunk": [
        {
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/LaurentSeries.tex",
          "line_range": [
            315,
            339
          ],
          "original_line_range": [
            316,
            326
          ],
          "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
            "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
            "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
          ],
          "labels": [
            "thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring"
          ],
          "snippet": "\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring}The $K$-module $K\\left[  x^{\\pm}\\right]  $,\nequipped with the multiplication we just defined, is a commutative\n$K$-algebra. Its unity is $\\left(  \\delta_{i,0}\\right)  _{i\\in\\mathbb{Z}}$.\nThe element $x$ is invertible in this $K$-algebra.\n\\end{theorem}\n\nThis $K$-algebra $K\\left[  x^{\\pm}\\right]  $ is called the \\emph{Laurent\npolynomial ring} in one indeterminate $x$ over $K$. It is often denoted by\n$K\\left[  x^{\\pm1}\\right]  $ or $K\\left[  x,x^{-1}\\right]  $ as well.\n\n\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.fps.laure.a=sumaixi}Any doubly infinite power series $a=\\left(\na_{i}\\right)  _{i\\in\\mathbb{Z}}\\in K\\left[  \\left[  x^{\\pm}\\right]  \\right]  $\nsatisfies%\n\\[\na=\\sum_{i\\in\\mathbb{Z}}a_{i}x^{i}.\n\\]\nHere, the powers $x^{i}$ are taken in the Laurent polynomial ring $K\\left[\nx^{\\pm}\\right]  $, but the infinite sum $\\sum_{i\\in\\mathbb{Z}}a_{i}x^{i}$ is\ntaken in the $K$-module $K\\left[  \\left[  x^{\\pm}\\right]  \\right]  $. (The\nnotions of summable families and infinite sums are defined in $K\\left[\n\\left[  x^{\\pm}\\right]  \\right]  $ in the same way as they are defined in\n$K\\left[  \\left[  x\\right]  \\right]  $.)\n\\end{proposition}\n"
        }
      ],

            "doc_excerpt": "*Laurent polynomials form a commutative K-algebra**.\n(Theorem thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring, label: thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring)\n\nThis is the key structural result: `K[T;T⁻¹]` is a commutative ring with `T` invertible.\ndef laurentPolynomial_commRing : CommRing K[T;T⁻¹] := inferInstance",
            "declaration_head": "/-- **Laurent polynomials form a commutative K-algebra**.\n(Theorem thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring, label: thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring)\n\nThis is the key structural result: `K[T;T⁻¹]` is a commutative ring with `T` invertible. -/\ndef laurentPolynomial_commRing : CommRing K[T;T⁻¹]",

            "referenced_parts": [],
            "doc_excerpt": "The variable `T` in Laurent polynomials is a unit (invertible).\n(Part of Theorem thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring)\ntheorem T_isUnit : IsUnit (T 1 : K[T;T⁻¹]) := by\nrefine ⟨⟨T 1, T (-1), ?_, ?_⟩, rfl⟩\n· have h := T_add (R := K) 1 (-1)\nsimp only [add_neg_cancel] at h\nexact h.symm\n· have h := T_add (R := K) (-1) 1\nsimp only [neg_add_cancel] at h\nexact h.symm",
            "declaration_head": "/-- The variable `T` in Laurent polynomials is a unit (invertible).\n(Part of Theorem thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring) -/\ntheorem T_isUnit : IsUnit (T 1 : K[T;T⁻¹])",
            "policy": "prior same-file declaration only; target declaration is not inspected"

            "declaration_head": "/-- The unity of K[x^±] is T 0 = (δ_{i,0})_{i∈ℤ}.\nThis is part of Theorem `thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring`. -/\ntheorem laurentPolynomial_one_eq_T_zero : (1 : K[T;T⁻¹]) = LaurentPolynomial.T 0",

            "doc_excerpt": "The element T 1 (corresponding to x) is invertible in K[T;T⁻¹].\nThis is part of Theorem `thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring`.\ntheorem laurentPolynomial_T_isUnit : IsUnit (LaurentPolynomial.T 1 : K[T;T⁻¹]) :=\nLaurentPolynomial.isUnit_T 1",

      "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:49-49 (open)\nopen scoped LaurentPolynomial Polynomial\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:50-50 (open)\nopen LaurentPolynomial HahnSeries\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:52-52 (section)\nnoncomputable section\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:54-54 (namespace)\nnamespace AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.Laurent\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:253-253 (section)\nsection LaurentPolynomialBasics\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:255-255 (variable)\nvariable {K : Type*} [CommRing K]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:90-96 (LaurentPoly)\n/-- **Definition of Laurent polynomials** (Definition def.fps.laure.laupol)\n\nA Laurent polynomial over K is an essentially finite family (aₙ)_{n∈ℤ}, represented\nin Mathlib as `LaurentPolynomial K = AddMonoidAlgebra K ℤ = ℤ →₀ K`.\n\nThis definition captures the key property: only finitely many coefficients are nonzero. -/\nabbrev LaurentPoly (K : Type*) [CommRing K] := K[T;T⁻¹]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:151-158 (laurentPoly_one_coeff)\n@[simp]\ntheorem laurentPoly_one_coeff (n : ℤ) :\n    (1 : LaurentPoly K) n = if n = 0 then 1 else 0 := by\n  show (T 0 : K[T;T⁻¹]) n = _\n  rw [T_apply]\n  by_cases h : n = := inferInstance\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/LaurentSeries.lean:265-274 (T_isUnit)\n/-- The variable `T` in Laurent polynomials is a unit (invertible).\n(Part of Theorem           "/-- **Laurent polynomials are the essentially finite families**.\n\nThis theorem explicitly states that Laurent polynomials (as `ℤ →₀ K`) are exactly\nthe essentially finite families (aₙ)_{n∈ℤ}, formalizing Definition def.fps.laure.laupol. -/\ntheorem laurentPoly_iff_essentiallyFinite (f : ℤ → K) :\n    (∃ p : LaurentPoly K, ∀ n, p n = f n) ↔ {n : ℤ | f n ≠ 0}.Finite := by\n  constructor\n  · intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n    have h := laurentPoly_support_finite p\n    have heq : {n : ℤ | f n ≠ 0} = {n : ℤ | p n ≠ 0} := by\n      ext n\n      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]\n      rw [hp n]\n    rw [heq]\n    exact h\n  · intro hf\n    refine ⟨⟨hf.toFinset, fun n => f n, ?_⟩, fun n => rfl⟩\n    intro n\n    simp only [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.mem_setOf_eq, ne_eq]\n\nend LaurentPolynomialDefinition\n\nsection LaurentPolynomialBasics\n\nvariable {K : Type*} [CommRing K]\n\n/-- **Laurent polynomials form a commutative K-algebra**.\n(Theorem thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring, label: thm.fps.laure.laupol-ring)\n\nThis is the key structural result: `K[T;T⁻¹]` is a commutative ring with `T` invertible. -/\ndef laurentPolynomial_commRing : CommRing K[T;T⁻¹] := inferInstance\n\ndef laurentPolynomial_algebra : Algebra K K[T;T⁻¹] := inferInstance",

In Mathlib, this is captured by the\nModule instance on LaurentPolynomial. -/\ndef laurentPoly_module : Module K (LaurentPoly K) := inferInstance",
